steps.title: User submits create form
steps.desc: Apollo Client fires the createDevice GraphQL mutation with a DeviceCreateInput payload. JWT in Authorization header.

steps.title: Resolver validates & writes
steps.desc: createDevice resolver in resolvers.ts calls requireAuth(), then prisma.device.create() with DEVICE_INCLUDE.

steps.title: Activity log written
steps.desc: If dateAcquired is set, a DEVICE_ACQUIRED ActivityLog row is inserted. This powers the dashboard activity feed.

steps.title: Device returned to client
steps.desc: Full Device object (with relations) returned. Apollo Client writes it to its normalised cache; UI re-renders.

steps.title: User saves changes
steps.desc: Apollo Client fires updateDevice mutation with a DeviceUpdateInput. Only changed fields are sent.

steps.title: Resolver reads current state
steps.desc: updateDevice resolver reads the existing device (status, functionalStatus, lastPowerOnDate) before applying the update — needed to detect what changed for activity logging.

steps.title: DB row updated
steps.desc: prisma.device.update() writes the clean data. Undefined fields are stripped so they don't overwrite existing values with null.

steps.title: Value snapshot created (conditional)
steps.desc: If estimatedValue changed, a ValueSnapshot row is inserted — but only if the value differs from the last snapshot. Powers the per-device value history chart.

steps.title: Activity log entries written
steps.desc: STATUS_CHANGED, FUNCTIONAL_STATUS_CHANGED, or POWERED_ON entries are inserted for each detected change. Powers the dashboard feed.

steps.title: User clicks Delete
steps.desc: deleteDevice(id) mutation fired from web Trash page or iOS device detail.

steps.title: Resolver sets deleted flag
steps.desc: prisma.device.update({ data: { deleted: true } }) — device is hidden from all queries but not removed from DB.

steps.title: User clicks Restore in Trash
steps.desc: restoreDevice(id) mutation fired from the /trash admin page.

steps.title: Resolver clears deleted flag
steps.desc: prisma.device.update({ data: { deleted: false } }). Full device object returned.

steps.title: User confirms permanent deletion
steps.desc: permanentlyDeleteDevice(id) mutation fired from the /trash admin page after confirmation dialog.

steps.title: API collects image paths
steps.desc: Resolver fetches device with { include: { images: true } } to get all file paths before deletion.

steps.title: DB cascade delete
steps.desc: prisma.device.delete() removes the row; Prisma cascades to images, notes, maintenanceTasks, tags, customFieldValues.

steps.title: Upload files deleted from disk
steps.desc: Resolver loops over image paths and calls fs.unlinkSync() on each. The device's upload directory is then removed.

steps.title: Client selects image file
steps.desc: Web: file input triggers. iOS: PHPickerViewController presents photo library. Image is held in memory ready to POST.

steps.title: POST /upload (multipart/form-data)
steps.desc: Client POSTs to /upload?deviceId=X. This is a REST endpoint — not GraphQL — handled by multer (2 GB limit). JWT required.

steps.title: File saved to /uploads
steps.desc: multer writes the file to /uploads/devices/{deviceId}/{uuid}.ext on disk. The path is returned in the JSON response.

steps.title: createImage mutation fired
steps.desc: Client passes the returned path to createImage GraphQL mutation. Resolver calls generateThumbnailForUpload() — sharp generates a 320×320 WebP thumbnail and saves it alongside the original.

steps.title: Image row saved to DB
steps.desc: prisma.image.create() writes path, thumbnailPath, dateTaken (from EXIF), isThumbnail, and deviceId. First IMAGE auto-becomes the device thumbnail.

flows.name: Upload Video
flows.path: Web / iOS → REST /upload → /uploads → createImage (async thumb) → PostgreSQL

steps.title: Client selects video file
steps.desc: Web: file input with video/* accept. iOS: PHPickerViewController with video media type.

steps.title: POST /upload
steps.desc: Client POSTs the video to /upload?deviceId=X. multer accepts mp4/mov/webm/avi up to 2 GB.

steps.title: File saved to /uploads
steps.desc: multer writes the video to /uploads/devices/{deviceId}/{uuid}.mp4 (or original ext). Path returned to client.

steps.title: createImage mutation — DB row created immediately
steps.desc: Resolver creates the image record instantly (mediaType: VIDEO, no thumbnailPath yet) so the client isn't blocked by ffmpeg processing.

steps.title: Async: ffmpeg generates thumbnail
steps.desc: setImmediate() runs generateVideoThumbnail() in the background: ffmpeg extracts frame at 1s, sharp converts to 320×320 WebP, ffprobe reads duration. The image row is updated when done.

steps.title: User clicks Delete on image
steps.desc: deleteImage(id) mutation fired from web device detail Photos tab or iOS Images tab.

steps.title: Resolver fetches paths then deletes DB row
steps.desc: deleteImage resolver reads the image record to get path and thumbnailPath, then calls prisma.image.delete().

steps.title: Files removed from disk
steps.desc: fs.unlinkSync() called on both the original file and the WebP thumbnail (if it exists).

steps.title: Note submitted
steps.desc: createNote(input) mutation fired from web device detail, iOS Notes tab, or MCP add_note tool.

steps.title: Resolver writes note
steps.desc: createNote resolver calls prisma.note.create() with content, date (auto-timestamped), and deviceId.

steps.title: Task submitted
steps.desc: createMaintenanceTask(input) mutation fired from web, iOS, or MCP add_maintenance_task tool. Input includes label, dateCompleted, notes, optional cost.

steps.title: Resolver writes task
steps.desc: prisma.maintenanceTask.create(). The cost field rolls up into totalMaintenanceCost on the financials page.

steps.title: User submits password
steps.desc: Client POSTs { password } to /auth/login. This is a REST endpoint — not GraphQL.

steps.title: API validates password
steps.desc: Express route compares against AUTH_PASSWORD env var using bcrypt. If AUTH_PASSWORD is not set, auth is disabled entirely.

steps.title: JWT tokens issued
steps.desc: API returns { accessToken (1h), refreshToken (7d) } signed with JWT_SECRET. Client stores tokens and attaches accessToken as Authorization: Bearer header on subsequent requests.

steps.title: Client detects expiring access token
steps.desc: Before the 1-hour access token expires, client POSTs { refreshToken } to /auth/refresh.

steps.title: API issues new access token
steps.desc: Express verifies the refresh token against JWT_SECRET and returns a new { accessToken }. Refresh token is not rotated.

steps.title: User uploads ZIP file
steps.desc: Backup page POSTs ZIP to /import (2 GB limit via multer diskStorage to /tmp/imports). Server returns { jobId } immediately — processing is async.

steps.title: API extracts and validates
steps.desc: ZIP is unpacked; manifest JSON parsed; device records validated. Errors are collected (fail-collect, not fail-fast) so partial imports succeed.

steps.title: Images written to /uploads
steps.desc: Each bundled image extracted to /uploads/devices/{id}/. sharp generates a 320×320 WebP thumbnail per image.

steps.title: Devices upserted to DB
steps.desc: prisma.device.upsert() called per device. Images inserted via prisma.image.createMany().

steps.title: Client polls for progress
steps.desc: Web polls GET /import/progress/:jobId. On completion, result includes { imported, skipped, errors }. Progress bar advances in real-time.

steps.title: User initiates export
steps.desc: Backup page POSTs { deviceIds } to /export/start. Server returns { jobId } immediately.

steps.title: API builds ZIP asynchronously
steps.desc: API reads device rows and image files from disk, bundles them into a ZIP with a manifest JSON. Progress tracked in exportJobs Map.

steps.title: Client polls progress
steps.desc: Web polls GET /export/progress/:jobId until status = 'done'.

steps.title: Client downloads ZIP
steps.desc: Browser navigates to GET /export/download/:jobId which streams the assembled ZIP file.

steps.title: Admin fills journey form
steps.desc: JourneyEditor.tsx in showcase/src/components/admin/ fires the createJourney mutation with { title, slug, description, published, sortOrder }.

steps.title: Resolver creates journey
steps.desc: createJourney resolver calls prisma.showcaseJourney.create(). Returns the new journey with its id.

steps.title: Admin list cache invalidated
steps.desc: createJourney mutation has refetchQueries: [GET_ALL_SHOWCASE_JOURNEYS_ADMIN] so the journey list page reflects the new entry immediately without a page refresh.

steps.title: Editor navigates to edit page
steps.desc: On success, router.push('/admin/journeys/{id}') takes the admin to the full editor where chapters and devices can be added.

steps.title: Editor loads journey data
steps.desc: GET_ALL_JOURNEYS_FOR_EDIT query fetches chapters and their showcase devices via Apollo. Cache-first, network if stale.

steps.title: Chapter title/description auto-saves on blur
steps.desc: ChapterCard.tsx fires upsertChapter mutation on input blur. Creates a new chapter if no id yet; updates if id exists.

steps.title: Resolver upserts chapter
steps.desc: prisma.showcaseChapter.upsert() keyed on id. sortOrder changes from reorder buttons are also persisted here.

steps.title: Device added to chapter
steps.desc: DeviceSearchModal fires upsertShowcaseDevice mutation with { chapterId, deviceId, sortOrder, isFeatured }.

steps.title: ShowcaseDevice row created
steps.desc: prisma.showcaseDevice.upsert() links the inventory Device to the chapter. curatorNote and isFeatured can be edited inline.

steps.title: Admin triggers export or import
steps.desc: GET /showcase/export streams a ZIP. POST /showcase/import receives a ZIP (up to 2 GB). Both endpoints require auth.

steps.title: Export: ZIP built from DB + uploads
steps.desc: API reads all journeys, chapters, and showcase devices from DB. Active showcase images are bundled into the ZIP.

steps.title: Import: ZIP extracted, DB upserted
steps.desc: Import unpacks ZIP, parses manifest, upserts journeys/chapters/devices by slug. Images written to /uploads. Missing inventory devices are silently skipped.

flows.name: AI Query (Chat)
flows.path: iOS / Web → OpenAI → MCP Server → API → PostgreSQL

steps.title: User types or speaks a question
steps.desc: iOS ChatView (SFSpeechRecognizer for voice input) or web CollectionChat sends the message to OpenAI with the MCP tool list attached.

steps.title: OpenAI selects an MCP tool
steps.desc: The model chooses from: list_all_devices, search_devices, get_device_details, get_financial_summary, list_devices, update_device, add_note, add_maintenance_task.

steps.title: MCP server calls GraphQL API
steps.desc: MCP server sends the appropriate GraphQL query or mutation to /graphql using a service JWT. Results serialised and returned as tool output.

steps.title: OpenAI composes response
steps.desc: Tool result fed back to OpenAI; model writes a natural-language reply. iOS streams text via AVSpeechSynthesizer if voice mode is on.

steps.title: Next.js SSR page loads
steps.desc: Storefront server-side fetches devices({ where: { status: FOR_SALE } }) via GraphQL. No auth token — storefront is fully public.

steps.title: API filters devices
steps.desc: devices resolver queries DB with status filter. Auth-sensitive fields (priceAcquired, notes, whereAcquired) are excluded from the response for unauthenticated requests.

steps.title: HTML rendered and served
steps.desc: Next.js renders the product grid to HTML. Umami analytics events fire in the browser when the user searches, filters, or sorts.

steps.title: Admin opens Usage page
steps.desc: orphanedFiles query fires lazily when the user opens the orphaned files section on /usage. Returns files on disk not referenced by any Image record.

steps.title: API scans /uploads against DB
steps.desc: Resolver walks /uploads directory tree and compares each file path against all Image.path and Image.thumbnailPath values in DB. Unreferenced paths returned as [OrphanedFile].

steps.title: Admin deletes selected files
steps.desc: deleteOrphanedFiles(paths) mutation called with selected paths. Resolver validates each path is inside /uploads before deletion.

steps.title: Files removed from disk
steps.desc: API calls fs.unlinkSync() on each validated path. Returns count of deleted files. No DB changes needed — these files had no DB record.

steps.title: Admin triggers generation
steps.desc: Web POSTs to /generate-image with { deviceId, prompt, model, sourceImageId? }. Returns { jobId } immediately.

steps.title: API calls OpenAI
steps.desc: If sourceImageId provided, the source image is converted to PNG and sent as an edit request. Otherwise a new generation is requested. Model is configurable (gpt-image-1.5 or gpt-image-2).

steps.title: Generated image saved to /uploads
steps.desc: OpenAI returns base64 image data. API decodes and writes to /uploads/devices/{deviceId}/{uuid}.png.

steps.title: Image record created in DB
steps.desc: createImage called internally; thumbnailPath generated via sharp. Showcase flag set if this is a showcase image.

steps.title: Client polls for completion
steps.desc: Web polls GET /generate-image/status/:jobId. On completion, the new image appears in the device's photo gallery.
